items.title: Tempest Low Intensity Power 31.5k/35.5k
items.cleaned_transcript:
For the three-button build, precast your elementals, then camp Overload Air. Any time you swap to an attunement outside of air, Fresh Air will fully refresh air attunement when you crit, so you should be able to swap back to air as soon as you exit air and keep spamming Overload Air. You bounce between fire and water as the cooldowns permit. More or less, you Overload Air, switch to fire, go back to air, Overload Air, switch to water, go back to air, and repeat.

For the five-button build, it is the same concept. You mostly camp Overload Air. Since you take Fresh Air, any time you crit outside of air, air attunement recharges, so you can go back to air and spam Overload Air. Swap between fire and water attunements as cooldowns allow.

The two added buttons are "Feel the Burn!" and weapon skill three. "Feel the Burn!" is a shout with no cast time, so you can cast it whenever and it will not interrupt anything, even during an overload. Just spam it if it is off cooldown. For weapon skill three, try to time your air skill three during your air-to-water-to-air swap. It is not absolutely necessary, so you can use it whenever it is up if you want, but never delay your overload for it. You also use skill three in fire. You can skip fire three if you want and only lose maybe 500 DPS, but if you want a little more damage, use fire skill three while going from fire back to air. Fire skill two and fire skill five are also good if you want to squeeze out more damage. Do not cast any of the water skills.

For the build rundown, elementalist has a lot of skills because of attunements, but the main focus is air. In water, do not cast anything. In fire, skills two, three, and five are good to cast. Air skill three is a little CC and also a blink, so it is a strong skill, but be careful not to blink into death. Air skill four, Ride the Lightning, is also a mobility skill. Air skill five is CC. Use skill five, and skill two if applicable, when there is a breakbar.

Trait-wise, there is not much to add. The build is mostly damage-oriented. Persisting Flames buffs fire fields, and this interacts with "Feel the Burn!" and fire skills. The big trait is Fresh Air: any time you are outside of air and crit, air attunement fully resets so you can go back to air. The build revolves around spamming air.

For the healing skill, you can run whatever you want. I use "Wash the Pain Away!" because I think it is one of the strongest elementalist healing skills for squads or parties. It heals your group, heals you, and cleanses conditions. "Feel the Burn!" should not be swapped. Signet of Fire should not be swapped because it gives precision. The elementals are not absolutely necessary if you do not want to run them, but there is not much better to take from a low-intensity perspective.

You can take "Rebound!" in some situations. It lets allies ignore a lethal hit, so it has niche uses, such as surviving a one-shot attack if you need that. Most of the time, stick with the elementals. Glyph of Lesser Elementals can be stacked before the fight. If you are waiting around before a fight, you can spawn several fire elementals to increase your damage a little. The elite elemental does not stack the same way. If you swap a utility skill, I recommend swapping Glyph of Lesser Elementals.

Elementalist has useful utility options. "Aftershock!" gives magnetic aura and can provide reflects, but be careful because sometimes you do not want to reflect. Frost Bow and Lightning Hammer used to be better, but from a low-intensity perspective I would avoid conjures because they are annoying to use.

To play this build as alacrity, take Lucid Singularity. You will need some boon duration, because the build is not quite enough by itself. I would look for about 40% to 50% boon duration. Diviner's gear is an option, but be careful to make sure you still crit cap. If you use Diviner's, you may need Assassin's gear to make up the lost precision. Superior Rune of Fireworks is another option and is an easy swap that gives boon duration without changing all your gear, though it loses some damage. Superior Sigil of Concentration is another option.

You already get some natural boon duration from Gathered Focus, which gives flat concentration. If you slot in Fireworks runes by themselves and do not want to swap to Diviner's gear, that may be enough. You can see alacrity stack naturally while playing the same way: overload, swap attunements, and continue. I recommend around 40% to 50% boon duration to be safe. The easiest low-effort swap is another Berserker set with Fireworks runes, and maybe a boon duration sigil on your sword. It is a damage loss, like all boon DPS builds, but then you can play it as DPS in squads without changing the rotation much.
